overview.about: The Colosseum in Rome, completed in 80 AD, is the largest ancient amphitheatre ever built. It held 50,000–80,000 spectators watching gladiatorial contests, animal hunts and public spectacles. Over one million animals and 400,000 people died within its walls. It remains the most iconic symbol of the Roman Empire.

highlights.label: Largest Amphitheatre Ever Built
highlights.desc: Seated up to 80,000 Romans — 50,000 tonnes of travertine limestone assembled over just 10 years of construction

highlights.label: 1,940 Years Old — Still Standing
highlights.desc: Completed 80 AD by Emperor Titus — witness to gladiatorial contests, naval battles and wild animal hunts for 4 centuries

highlights.label: UNESCO World Heritage Icon
highlights.desc: The most photographed ancient structure on Earth — the Colosseum at sunset is one of Rome's most powerful images

highlights.label: Hypogeum — The Underground Maze
highlights.desc: The labyrinthine tunnels beneath the arena floor where gladiators and animals awaited their fate are now open to visit

overview.about: Trevi Fountain in Rome, IT sits at the very heart of Western civilisation — a testament to the ambition, engineering and artistry of ancient and Renaissance Italy. Walking through this storied heritage landmark site is a living lesson in the history that shaped the entire modern world.

highlights.label: Centuries of Living History
highlights.desc: Structures standing for hundreds to thousands of years — each stone a silent chronicle of empires and civilisations

highlights.label: UNESCO World Heritage Site
highlights.desc: Recognised globally for outstanding universal value and protected as one of Earth's most significant cultural landmarks

highlights.label: Iconic Photography Spot
highlights.desc: Panoramic vistas, intricate carvings and sunrise silhouettes that appear on bucket lists worldwide — every angle tells a story

highlights.label: Architectural Masterpiece
highlights.desc: Precision engineering predating modern tools — symmetry, scale and craftsmanship that continues to baffle experts today
